steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left is an independent clause ending with “to die,” and on the right is an independent clause beginning with “it left unexplored.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses joined by a coordinating conjunction require a comma before the conjunction. 'But' is the coordinating conjunction here, so choice B correctly includes the comma before 'but.' The other choices omit the required comma, omit the conjunction altogether, or use only a comma without a conjunction. That matches choice B — B is correct.
